target.title: webchat creates new agent run per message, destroying prompt cache (93% → 29% hit rate)
target.body:
## Summary

Each user message in webchat spawns a **new embedded agent run** (new runId), rebuilding the system prompt on every turn. This destroys provider prompt-cache prefix stability. Cache hit rate plummets from 93% (Feishu, stable) to 29% (webchat).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Send a message via webchat
2. Send a second message immediately via the same webchat session
3. Compare cache hit rate between the two API requests

## Expected Behavior

Within the same session, the second request should have equal or higher cache hit rate. The system prompt and first message are already in the provider cache.

## Actual Behavior

The second webchat request has a **much lower** cache hit rate than the first (85.8% → 31.4%). Meanwhile, the same test via Feishu on the same OpenClaw instance shows stable 98% for both requests.

## Detailed Data

### Hello Test Comparison

Two identical "hello" messages sent in sequence, webchat vs Feishu:

| Channel | Req 1 Δtokens | Req 1 Δmiss | Req 1 Hit% | Req 2 Δtokens | Req 2 Δmiss | Req 2 Hit% |
|---------|-------------|-----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|-----------|
| **webchat** | 245,116 | 34,831 | **85.8%** | 244,573 | 167,746 | **31.4%** ⚠️ |
| **Feishu** | 31,863 | 592 | **98.1%** | 69,914 | 1,028 | **98.5%** ✅ |

- webchat Req 2 miss is **5× higher** than Req 1 (34K → 168K)
- Feishu: both requests stable at ~98% hit rate

### Full-Day Data (same OpenClaw instance)

| Period / Channel | Total Tokens | Cache Miss | Hit Rate |
|-----------------|-------------|-----------|---------|
| Day (Feishu) | ~31.5M | ~2.12M | **93.2%** |
| Evening (webchat) | ~3M | ~2.13M | **29.0%** |

## Root Cause

Gateway logs confirm that webchat creates a **new embedded agent run with a new runId for every message** (sessionId stays the same):

```
embedded run start: runId=d67a8e97... sessionId=11af5b93... webchat  ← msg 1
embedded run start: runId=3935414f... sessionId=11af5b93... webchat  ← msg 2 (new runId)
embedded run start: runId=fbf48e8c... sessionId=11af5b93... webchat  ← msg 3 (new runId)
```

Each new run rebuilds the system prompt and sends it as a fresh request to the provider. Even if the system prompt text is byte-identical, the repeated rebuild invalidates the provider prompt-cache prefix continuity.

Feishu is unaffected: messages arrive via channel webhook → reuse the same agent run → no system prompt rebuild → cache accumulates → 93%+.

## Related

- #84110 Same pattern on Codex (tool-call continuation rewrites prompt, cache 93% → 47%)
- #87998 Fixed similar issue where media task hints were placed above CACHE_BOUNDARY
- #80855 WebChat session handling

## Environment

- OpenClaw: v2026.5.28
- Provider: anthropic-messages compatible API (reproduced on both MiniMax portal and DeepSeek)
- OS: macOS (arm64)
target.author: Enominera
